secondary_archetype: Technical PM with sales/marketing tech flavor

level_signals: Senior Product Manager with 4+ years of experience with technical products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Senior Product Manager with 4+ years of experience with technical products
Ability to actively manage competing priorities, requirements and meet deadlines
Ability to develop a 360° view of Business, Technology, and UX perspective

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: even better if you have experience in sales/marketing tech!

scope.surface: AI-powered B2B sales platform (lead generation, hyper-personalized sequencing, omnichannel outreach, AI smart actions)
core_responsibilities: Help define product vision, strategy, roadmap, and growth opportunities, identifying areas of improvement
Work hands-on with the development, design and customer-facing teams, participating in stand-ups, providing feedback, and assisting in testing
Keep track of industry trends, diving into product and customer discovery
Ensure new products and features are evaluated for usability and performance, employing focus group testing when necessary

green_flags: YCombinator-backed with named customers (Deel, Moveworks, H1, Vanta)
Post-Series A with stated product-market fit
Inclusive hiring language and commitment to diversity
red_flags: Compensation range not disclosed — only 'Competitive Salary' mentioned
JD is relatively thin on specifics — few measurable outcomes or success criteria defined
No explicit mention of team size or reporting structure

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred as Senior IC from title and years requirement)
location.type (inferred as remote-global from 'Nice remote work environment' — no cities or regions stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and Series A mention)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation.source set to unknown because no salary range is given
ambiguities: Location is described only as 'Nice remote work environment' with no country or region restrictions specified — assumed remote-global but could be limited to specific regions not disclosed
Visa sponsorship not mentioned — marked as unclear
The four must-have bullets appear in a 'Who we're looking for' section without explicit 'required' language, but are treated as hard requirements given unconditional phrasing
